ops.text:
E23 FINAL RESULT (resume lane, finished from disk). All 14 candidate rules judged (R14 completed this lane: 1 opus/spawnllm-large call, same RUBRIC/protocol as R01-R13; n=8 held-out sites; correct=1 fa=7 -> prec=0.125). _summary.json rebuilt from all 14 judged files; scripts/finalize.py produced out/precision_table.md + bootstrap CIs (B=5000, seed=23) and out/rules.json (top-level criterion block).

PRE-REGISTERED CRITERION (evaluated exactly as registered): >=10 rules at >=80% judged precision. RESULT: n_passing=3, criterion_met=FALSE.

Precision table (point est / 95% CI, n=8 each):
 R10-bare-great-docs-build  100% [1.0,1.0]  PASS (gate)
 R12-git-mutation            88% [0.625,1.0] PASS (gate)
 R13-raw-grep-on-source      88% [0.625,1.0] PASS (nudge)
 R11-install-adds-dependency 25% [0.0,0.625]
 R04-cast-to-quiet-checker   12% [0.0,0.375]
 R06-tooling-meta-comment    12% [0.0,0.375]
 R07-fallback-compat-shim    12% [0.0,0.375]
 R08-hasattr-on-typed        12% [0.0,0.375]
 R09-pipe-ccx-head-tail      12% [0.0,0.375]
 R14-loose-scratch-md        12% [0.0,0.375]
 R01-type-ignore-added        0% [0.0,0.0]
 R02-bare-except-swallow      0% [0.0,0.0]
 R03-mutable-default-arg      0% [0.0,0.0]
 R05-widen-to-any             0% [0.0,0.0]

INTERPRETATION (TRACE-style mechanical-compilation hypothesis). The 14 candidates split cleanly. (a) The only high-precision rules are those whose trigger is a near-unique COMMAND signature with essentially no legitimate instance: R10 (literal `great-docs build`), R12 (git commit/push in a jj-colocated world), R13 (raw grep/rg on source). (b) Every CONTENT-regex rule over Edit/Write payloads (R01-R08) and the .md path-suffix rule (R14) collapses to 0-12.5%, and the two failing command rules (R09 head/tail pipe, R11 install) to 12-25%. Cause is not that the underlying corrections are wrong -- it is that the MECHANICAL trigger has no access to the context that decides whether the pattern is a problem HERE: `# type: ignore`/cast/Any/except-Exception fire overwhelmingly in test fixtures that deliberately trigger errors, third-party/monkey-patch boundaries, and codegen (R01 0/8 all such); R14's .md hits are almost all harness-managed stores OUTSIDE the repo tree (cc-pool memory, ~/.claude/plans, /tmp) that the filename regex can't exclude (7/8 fa); R11 fires on re-installs of already-declared deps / dev tooling. Precision floors at the base rate of truly-problematic instances among syntactic matches (~0-25%).

IMPLICATION: Mechanical compilation of corrections into always-on capt-hook triggers is viable ONLY for the small minority with unique command signatures (3/14 here). The majority require an LLM-GATED hook (capt-hook nudge/gate with an LLM condition that reads the site context -- is this a test? a boundary? a file outside the tree? a genuinely-new dep?); the mechanical regex is a cheap PREFILTER deciding whether to invoke the LLM condition, not the decision itself (consistent with E1's finding that the lexical gate sheds almost nothing -- prefilter, not decider). A mechanical `# type: ignore` hook here would be 100% noise and train dismissal.

INSTRUMENT CAVEAT: n=8/rule -> wide CIs. Only R10's CI lower bound (1.0) clears 0.80; R12/R13 dip to 0.625, so even the passing set is fragile -- strengthens the negative. Also R10's sample is half in-train (only 4 held-out sessions existed); verdicts are per-site-context so 100% holds either way.

SPEND: this lane ~$0.011 (R14 judge, 1 opus call, 4804 in / 822 out chars); prior lane ~$0.21 (R01-R13); total ~$0.22, far under both the $15 pre-reg cap and this lane's $3 cap. ARTIFACTS (raw, for verify lane): /Users/yasyf/.cc-steer/experiments/E23/out/{rules.json,precision_table.md}; data/judged/{R01..R14}.json + _summary.json (+ _summary.R01-R13.bak.json backup of prior state); data/fired/R01..R14.json; scripts/{build_rules,replay,judge,finalize}.py.
